results.title: Guitar
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Video School of Music Series”.
With this series you can be taught professionally in your own home. You can replay difficult sections and of course have the same lesson over and over again. This video takes you through the basics of guitar playing with Steve Crofts, a protege of George Goller. Rated: G

results.title: Hellfire Pass
results.creator_credit: Graham Chase, Geoffrey Barnes and Film Australia
results.brief_description: On Anzac Day 1987, Sir Edmund “Weary” Dunlop and a group of ex-servicemen from Australia, Britain and Holland returned to the infamous Thai-Burma railway to dedicate Hellfire Pass as a monument to those who died. This film is their journey.

results.title: Looking into paintings 1-6
results.creator_credit: Charles Mapleston and Arts Council of Great Britain
results.brief_description: In this program Robert Cumming, Director of the Christie Fine Art Course, Norbert Lynton, Professor of Fine Art at Sussex University, and Alistair Smith, Keeper at the National gallery, help us look into paintings rather than just at them. They take us through some famous paintings, sometimes with the help of other experts, always with new ways of approaching the pictures, and with added insights, from contemporary artists, into the art of painting.

results.title: Niki Lauda Explains Formula One
results.brief_description: World racing car champion, Niki Lauda, demonstrates the sheer speed and performance of Formula One driving. Historical footage is used to explore the sport’s development from early days to recent times, including how performance has improved and records are consistently broken. Driving his McLaren, Lauda shows his skills, while animation illustrates the finer technical points,. Designed not only for the general viewer but also the motor racing enthusiast. Rated: G

results.title: Swing Fitness for Older Adults
results.brief_description: This gentle, all new, swing movement video is designed specifically for the young at heart (30-80 years) who wish to keep supple, healthy and full of life with a minimum of effort and a maximum amount of enjoyment. If you are ready to begin exercising in the safest way possible, you are ready for this remarkable program.

results.title: Small Scale Vegetable Growing
results.brief_description: James Murison, Australian horticulturist, presents a comprehensive guide from site selection to long term storage of your vegetable crop and includes information on soil testing, drainage, fertilisers, pest and disease control and crop rotation.

results.title: Carving Techniques and Projects
results.brief_description: Demonstrates the basics of gouge and chisel work, discussing the choice of tools, ideas and how to adjust designs. Shows how to carve the hallmark of 18th century furniture: the classic scallop shell.

results.title: Carve a Ball and Claw Foot
results.brief_description: Learn how to scale the ball-and-claw to furniture plan, how to machine stock to yield a graceful bandsaw cabriole leg ready for carving, how to lay out form, cut-in with a mallet and gouge and shape the dragon’s claw for a vigorous looking grip, how to carve efficiently, keeping tools sharp as you work.
results.description: Learn how to scale the ball-and-claw to furniture plan, how to machine stock to yield a graceful bandsaw cabriole leg ready for carving, how to lay out form, cut-in with a mallet and gouge and shape the dragon’s claw for a vigorous looking grip, how to carve efficiently, keeping tools sharp as you work.

results.title: Police State
results.creator_credit: ABC-TV (Australia), Rod Allan and Chris Noonan
results.brief_description: Police State is the story of corruption in the Queensland police force and body politic, realised through drama re-enactments, twenty years of news and documentary footage, and a compelling narrative. In the final years of the Bjelke-Petersen administration, Queensland has become as close to a police state as anything since the convict era. “Police State” puts into perspective the headlines which have dominated the Australian media for the past two years. Rated: PG
